Defendant City And County Of San Franciscos Notice Of Motion And Motion To Consolidate
Off Calendar Nov-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Consolidate.

Defendant City and County Of San Francisco's motion to consolidate case CGC-20-588665 (the "Burchell action" and CGC-21-590462 (the "Reynolds" action) for all purposes is granted. The Court designates case CGC-20-588665 as the lead case and all future filings shall be made under that case number.

Under Code of Civil Procedure section 1048, the Court has broad discretion to consolidate actions with common questions of law or fact. (National Elec. Supply Co. v. Mt. Diablo Unified School Dist. (1960) 187 Cal.App.2d 418, 421.) In this case, it is undisputed the two actions involve nearly identical questions of law and fact and arise out of the City and County of San Francisco's alleged negligent conduct in connection with the Pier 45 and Shed C fire on May 23, 2020. In fact, the legal claims stated by the 34 plaintiffs in their respective complaints are identical. (Murphy Decl. 5.) Additionally, both actions are in the early phases of similar discovery. For example, Defendant plans to provide the Reynolds Plaintiff with all documents and discovery produced in the Burchell action. (Murphy Decl. 14.) Plaintiffs in the Burchell action have not shown that consolidation is so unduly prejudicial it outweighs the factors favoring consolidation, including the promotion of judicial economy by avoiding duplication of discovery. Therefore, consolidation for all purposes including discovery and trial is proper to avoid unnecessary inefficiencies and the risk of inconsistent adjudication if ligated separately. (In re Marriage of Davis (1977) 68 Cal.App.3d 294, 304 ["The law abhors multiplicity of actions, consuming the time of the court and entailing additional expense to the people."]).

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
